summary: US-Iran diplomatic efforts are underway via Pakistani mediation, though tensions remain high over uranium enrichment and Hormuz Strait tolls. Israel continues operations in Southern Lebanon, suffering casualties from drone strikes despite a nominal ceasefire. US Treasury has intensified pressure on Hezbollah by designating nine high-ranking political and security officials.
key_points: US and Iran are negotiating via Pakistani mediators to end military conflict, though internal US divisions persist (Source: @VahidOnline/75597).
Iran's Supreme Leader has ordered that near-weapons-grade uranium must remain within Iranian borders (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2861).
IDF suffered multiple casualties in Southern Lebanon, including one female soldier seriously wounded by a drone bomb (Source: @lelotsenzura/92026).
Iran established the Persian Gulf Strait Administration (PGSA) to create a controlled maritime zone in Hormuz (Source: @kianmeli1/87523).
US Treasury designated nine Hezbollah-aligned officials in Lebanon and Iran to obstruct the group's operations (Source: @shin_persian/6123).
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that Iranian transit tolls in Hormuz would make diplomacy impossible (Source: @VahidOnline/75597).
Israeli airstrikes targeted the Lebanese town of Tebnine, reportedly violating the current ceasefire (Source: @presstv/190834).
US Representative Tom Cole warned DoD funding for Iran operations may be exhausted by August (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2863).

sections.executive_summary: US-Iran diplomatic negotiations are currently active via Pakistani mediation, though the US warns of military options if a 'good deal' is not reached. Israel is facing operational friction in Southern Lebanon, with drone strikes causing multiple IDF casualties. The US has shifted toward targeted financial warfare by sanctioning key Hezbollah political and security figures.

points: The Supreme Leader and Iranian establishment have reached a consensus that near-weapons-grade uranium must remain inside Iran, rejecting proposals for its removal (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2861).
Iran has formally announced the 'Persian Gulf Strait Administration' (PGSA) and established a controlled maritime zone within the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @kianmeli1/87523).
Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that Iran's military structure used the one-month ceasefire period to rebuild operational capabilities (Source: @kianmeli1/87515).
The IRGC Navy's Deputy Commander threatened that forces capable of disrupting the Strait of Hormuz remain operational despite US claims of their destruction (Source: @lelotsenzura/92027).
Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is maintaining diplomatic channels with European counterparts, including Austria, to discuss regional developments (Source: @presstv/190843).
The IDF reported one female soldier seriously wounded and four others moderately to lightly wounded following a drone bomb attack in Southern Lebanon (Source: @lelotsenzura/92026).
Prime Minister Netanyahu expressed frustration to President Trump regarding Iranian 'time-wasting' tactics during diplomatic negotiations (Source: @VahidOnline/75586).
The IDF Chief of Staff confirmed that the military remains on high alert across all fronts during the Shavuot holiday (Source: @inon_yttach/8226).
Israeli intelligence and military leadership are coordinating with the US on 'hardline' policies against Iran to protect sensitive facilities (Source: @VahidOnline/75586).
IDF air alerts were activated in the Meshgav Am area due to the penetration of an enemy aircraft (Source: @idf_telegram/23770).
IDF forces arrested a suspect in the village of Huwara following a suspected ramming attempt near the Tapuach junction in the Samaria Brigade area (Source: @idf_telegram/23772).
The US Treasury designated nine Hezbollah-aligned officials, including Mohamed Abdel-Motteleb Fanich and Hassan Nizammedine Fadlallah, freezing their US-based assets (Source: @shin_persian/6123).
Israeli airstrikes targeted the southern town of Tebnine, which local sources claim is a violation of the ceasefire (Source: @presstv/190834).
Hezbollah released footage of a 'diving drone' attack targeting an IDF Iron Dome battery in the Shomera settlement (Source: @naya_foriraq/75825).
Artillery shelling was reported in the areas of Ain Baal and Shokin, with warnings issued for maximum caution (Source: @redlinkleb/15142).
US sanctions targeted Lebanese security officials, including Brigadier General Khattar Nasser Eldin and Colonel Samir Hamadi, for sharing intelligence with Hezbollah (Source: @shin_persian/6123).
UAE Senior Advisor Anwar Gargash stated that Iranian behavior in recent years has significantly eroded trust among Gulf Cooperation Council countries (Source: @VahidOnline/75594).
Leaders of Saudi Arabia and Qatar have expressed a preference for avoiding a return to open conflict during discussions with US envoys (Source: @VahidOnline/75586).

key_developments: US and Iran are engaged in negotiations via Pakistani mediators, including a visit by Army Chief Asim Munir to Tehran (Source: @VahidOnline/75597).
Iran's Supreme Leader has explicitly forbidden the removal of enriched uranium from Iranian territory (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2861).
The US Treasury sanctioned nine key Hezbollah figures across political, military, and Lebanese state security sectors (Source: @shin_persian/6123).
IDF suffered five casualties in Southern Lebanon due to a drone bomb, including one female soldier in critical condition (Source: @lelotsenzura/92026).
Iran established the Persian Gulf Strait Administration (PGSA) to exert control over the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @kianmeli1/87523).
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that Iranian transit tolls in Hormuz would terminate diplomatic possibilities (Source: @VahidOnline/75597).
US Representative Tom Cole indicated that DoD funding for Iran-related operations may run out by August (Source: @Faytuks_Network/2863).
sections.threat_assessment: The risk of kinetic escalation remains high despite diplomatic overtures. Iran's establishment of the PGSA and the Supreme Leader's stance on uranium suggest a strategy of maintaining maximum leverage. The IDF's vulnerability to drone attacks in Lebanon indicates a persistent threat to ground forces even during ceasefire periods.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states are split, with the UAE and Israel favoring hardline pressure on Iran, while Saudi Arabia and Qatar prioritize stability and the avoidance of renewed conflict. Lebanon's state security is further compromised as the US identifies high-ranking officials leaking intelligence to Hezbollah.
sections.intelligence_notes: Internal US friction is evident between the State/Defense departments (Rubio/Hegseth) and the VP/Envoys (Vance/Kushner) regarding the efficacy of pressure versus negotiation. The use of Pakistan as a mediator indicates a shift in diplomatic channels. The IRGC's focus on the Strait of Hormuz remains a primary strategic pressure point.
